label: Scalable neural quantum states architecture for quantum chemistry
paraphrase: Paper node for Zhao Stokes and Veerapaneni scalable quantum chemistry NQS.

label: Large molecules create local-energy and sampling scalability bottlenecks
paraphrase: The paper identifies two sources of NQS scaling difficulty in molecular systems.

label: MADE-based complex autoregressive molecular NQS
paraphrase: The method adapts MADE to complex-valued molecular wavefunctions with constraints.

label: GPU tensorized local-energy computation and distributed batches
paraphrase: The paper parallelizes local-energy computation by tensorizing Hamiltonian information on GPUs.

label: Reverse autoregressive sampling uses Hamiltonian/encoding structure
paraphrase: The paper improves sampling by ordering qubits according to Jordan-Wigner structure.

label: Quantum chemistry molecules up to 76 qubits and millions of Hamiltonian terms
paraphrase: The method is evaluated on molecular systems up to 76 qubits.

label: Algorithm scales to 76 qubits and achieves state-of-the-art performance
paraphrase: The paper reports strong molecular energy performance and scaling.

label: Large batches improve performance and parallelization gives near-optimal weak scaling
paraphrase: The ablations support the batch-size and weak-scaling design logic.

label: Autoregressive neural-network wavefunctions for ab initio quantum chemistry
paraphrase: Paper node for Barrett Malyshev and Lvovsky NAQS chemistry study.

label: Quantum chemistry has nonlocal interactions and high sample complexity
paraphrase: The paper frames electronic-structure NNQS as needing bespoke architecture and sampling.

label: RBM-based NNQS suffer inefficient sampling and weak molecular priors
paraphrase: The paper identifies RBM MCMC sampling and black-box priors as shortcomings.

label: Autoregressive neural network ansatz for second-quantized molecular wavefunctions
paraphrase: The method uses autoregressive factorization for molecular wavefunctions.

label: Sampling cost scales with unique configuration strings
paraphrase: The sampling method avoids redundant repeated configurations in large batches.

label: Physical priors are hard-coded into the autoregressive ansatz
paraphrase: The architecture enforces electron-number and symmetry constraints before training.

label: Molecules up to 30 spin-orbitals
paraphrase: The paper evaluates second-quantized NAQS on molecular systems up to 30 spin-orbitals.

label: NAQS approaches or matches FCI and can beat coupled cluster in strong-correlation regimes
paraphrase: The paper reports strong molecular ground-state performance including hard correlation regimes.

label: NAQS scales beyond previous second-quantized NNQS chemistry results
paraphrase: The paper reports improved scale and performance over prior RBM chemistry NNQS.

label: Local-energy evaluation becomes the remaining scaling barrier
paraphrase: The paper states that VMC local-energy cost limits further scaling once sampling improves.

label: Use network-isolated determinant subspaces for hybrid numerical methods
paraphrase: The paper suggests diagonalizing the subspace found by the autoregressive network.

label: Autoregressive neural quantum states with quantum number symmetries
paraphrase: Paper node for symmetry-aware ANQS.

label: Standard autoregressive sampling makes quantum-number symmetries hard to incorporate
paraphrase: The paper frames symmetry-compliant autoregressive sampling as the key challenge.

label: Postselecting symmetry sectors wastes compute
paraphrase: The paper explains why postselection is inefficient for ANQS.

label: Polynomial physicality check prunes unphysical autoregressive subtrees
paraphrase: The method makes autoregressive sampling symmetry-aware by pruning impossible partial samples.

label: Use molecular Z2 spatial symmetries with particle number and spin projection
paraphrase: The paper adds spatial Z2 symmetries to molecular ANQS.

label: Electronic-structure calculations across multiple molecules
paraphrase: The paper showcases the method on molecular electronic structure calculations.

label: Symmetry-aware ANQS reaches chemical accuracy for all studied molecules with order-of-magnitude speedup
paraphrase: The paper reports chemical accuracy and large speedups relative to previous ANQS results.

label: DUS pruning can lose samples and stall larger-molecule optimization
paraphrase: The paper identifies a practical sampling risk for one pruning strategy.
